name: Ministorum Sermon

verdict.issue: Core mechanic is correct: condition gates on unit-has-keyword MINISTORUM PRIEST, effect adds 1 to wound roll for melee attacks. Scope and duration are appropriate.

reasoning: Draft applied keyword-grant Sustained Hits 1 which is an entirely different mechanic. Correct encoding is a roll-modifier on wound rolls, narrowed to attack_type:melee (fixing the all-weapons gap), wrapped in a unit-has-keyword MINISTORUM PRIEST condition (fixing the missing membership gate).

name: Cherub

verdict.issue: Core mechanic (free Command Re-roll stratagem) is captured. The rule has a second clause — 'can do so even if you have already targeted a different unit with that Stratagem this phase' — which is a meaningful exception that grant_type:command-reroll-free may not encode. Secondary detail, not a wrong core.

reasoning: Rule text describes using Command Re-roll Stratagem for 0CP once per battle, bypassing the one-unit-per-phase restriction; modeled as ability-grant grant_type:command-reroll-free gated by once-per-battle timing. Gap description (Miracle dice / Act of Faith trigger) conflicts with the explicit rule text — encoding follows rule text, which is unambiguous; the gap description appears to be mislabeled from a different Sisters wargear ability.

name: Simulacrum Imperialis

verdict.issue: The rule is a flat Leadership +1 stat improvement. The authored DSL models a completely different mechanic: a miracle-dice resource gain gated on command phase + controls-objective. Wrong effect type, wrong stat/resource, and both conditions are phantom (absent from the rule entirely).

reasoning: Gap explicitly says the draft (Ld+1 stat-modifier) is completely wrong and the real effect is Miracle dice generation when controlling an objective; encoding follows the gap as authoritative. The provided rule text (Ld boost) appears mislabeled from a different ability. Trigger modeled as phase-is:command AND controls-objective, reflecting the standard Acts of Faith generation timing.

name: Attached Unit

verdict.issue: The Scouts 6" grant when a MINISTORUM PRIEST leader is attached is correctly modeled. Two secondary gaps: (1) the attachment-eligibility clause ('can be attached to this unit instead of BATTLE SISTERS SQUAD') is not represented; (2) the 'during the Declare Battle Formations step' timing constraint is missing — is-attached is a state check, not a timing gate.

reasoning: Draft's vague ability-grant missed both required conditions: the unit must be in an attached state AND the attached leader must carry the MINISTORUM PRIEST keyword. The encodable core effect is movement-modifier scouts:6 on the attached model. The attachment-eligibility redirect ('can attach to this unit instead of BATTLE SISTERS SQUAD') is a pre-game formation declaration not expressible in the effect grammar; the in-battle Scouts 6" grant is the substantive half and is fully encodable.

name: Atttached Unit

verdict.issue: Same shape as attached-unit but correctly uses OR to cover MINISTORUM PRIEST and INQUISITOR, matching the rule. Same secondary gaps: attachment-eligibility clause absent; Declare Battle Formations timing not gated. Core mechanic (Scouts 6" on either leader type) is correct.

reasoning: Structurally identical to attached-unit but the attached-leader keyword check expands to a compound or-condition covering MINISTORUM PRIEST | INQUISITOR, matching the rule's dual-leader eligibility. Scouts 6" is the encodable in-battle effect; the attachment eligibility redirect remains outside the effect grammar for the same reason as the sibling ability.

name: Leader

verdict.issue: Conditions (unit-has-keyword CHARACTER + model-is-leader) and effect (ability-grant:attach-to-bodyguard-unit) faithfully capture the rule. 'Before the battle' timing is implicitly handled by duration:permanent on a setup-phase ability; no phantom conditions or wrong mechanics.

reasoning: Draft lost the CHARACTER + Leader double constraint that gates attachment eligibility. Correct encoding adds both as an and-condition, then grants the attach-to-bodyguard-unit formation capability. Declare Battle Formations is a defined game step (not army-construction or roster selection), so this is encodable; the Bodyguard-unit type restriction and the 'form an Attached unit' semantics live in the grant_type label where downstream consumers resolve them.

name: Unstoppable Champion

verdict.issue: Core mechanic is correct (D6 ≥ 2 gates resurrection to 1 wound with correct placement constraints). The 'first time' frequency limit is encoded as a second 'timing-is: once-per-battle' operand — structurally wrong type (timing-is is a moment, not an occurrence count), but the intent and core effect are right.

reasoning: Draft already had correct roll structure and wounds_remaining; extended the resurrection modifier with placement:'as-close-as-possible' and placement_constraint:'not-within-engagement-range' to capture the gap. resurrection's modifier is not in the canonical list so best-effort keys are used — nothing is dropped.

name: Firing Deck 2

verdict.issue: The rule triggers 'each time this TRANSPORT shoots' (a per-shoot-action event), but the authored models it as a phase-is:shooting condition. The key mechanic — up to 2 embarked models from units that haven't shot — is captured in the grant_type string. Trigger granularity is off, core mechanic is intact.

reasoning: No grammar primitive exists for borrowing weapons from embarked models; ability-grant with a single descriptive grant_type encodes all three mechanic facets (weapon selection, capacity-2, units-not-shot-this-phase constraint) without losing any to a silent no-op. The phase+keyword condition gates it correctly to each shooting activation.

name: Rapid Deployment

verdict.issue: Condition (advanced-this-turn in movement phase), normal-move-consumed, and cannot-declare-charge all faithfully match the rule. The implicit 'can otherwise act normally' is the absence of restrictions and does not need to be modeled positively.

reasoning: Sequence of three leaves maps one-to-one onto the three rule clauses: ability-grant(disembark-after-advance) gives the permission, movement-modifier(normal-move-consumed) marks movement as spent, and attack-restriction(cannot-declare-charge) blocks the charge declaration — all gated on the transport having advanced this turn.

name: Fights First

verdict.issue: The fight-first effect is correct, but the rule's key qualifier — 'provided every model in the unit has this ability' — is collapsed into unit-has-keyword, missing the per-model check. This matters for Attached units where the character lacks Fights First; the condition would incorrectly pass.

reasoning: No all-models predicate exists in the grammar; unit-has-keyword:'Fights First' is the correct proxy — a pure unit trivially satisfies it, and any leader attachment that mixes in a model without the ability strips the unit-level keyword, naturally suppressing the fight-first leaf. This is the only available encoding that captures the qualification without inventing keys.

name: Lone Operative

verdict.issue: NOT(is-attached) condition is correct. The ranged targeting restriction with 12" attacker proximity threshold matches the rule exactly.

reasoning: not(is-attached) directly and cleanly encodes 'unless part of an Attached unit'; the attack-restriction leaf encodes the 12-inch ranged-targeting window. Draft had the restriction right but omitted the is-attached negation entirely.

name: Acrobatic Escape

verdict.issue: Two errors: (1) The Fall Back move is 'up to D6"' but authored encodes it as a flat value:6 — omitting the random distance is a core mechanic error. (2) Step 3 fires unconditionally at end-of-battle; the rule requires the model to be off the battlefield for destruction to apply ('if this model is not on the battlefield') — the authored destroys it regardless, which is a phantom condition.

reasoning: Sequence encodes all three rule clauses: step 1 — end-of-fight-phase fall-back gated on engagement-state (D6 distance approximated as value:6, the maximum); step 2 — end-of-opponent-turn removal+redeploy encoded as deep-strike (9-inch placement constraint is standard to that leaf), gated on not(opponent-unit-within-range:3); step 3 — end-of-battle model-destruction. Limitation: 'model is not on the battlefield' is inexpressible in the condition grammar, so step 3 is gated on timing alone — a consuming implementation must track off-board state separately.

name: Lord of Deceit (Aura)

verdict.issue: Core mechanic correct: condition gates on the targeted unit being within 12", effect increases CP cost by 1. scope.range aura-12 and duration battle match the rule. 'resource-spend' on 'enemy-within-aura' with cp+1 is an acceptable encoding of 'opponent pays 1 more CP'.

reasoning: The 12" proximity is credited in scope.range; opponent-unit-within-range is the closest available trigger for the stratagem-use event (no dedicated stratagem-use condition type exists); resource-spend on enemy-within-aura models forcing the opponent to pay 1 extra CP for that stratagem use, replacing the cryptic target-has-keyword A condition and the incorrect cp-gain direction.

name: Shadow Assignment

verdict.issue: The rule has two stated clauses: (1) this model cannot be selected as WARLORD, and (2) faction-gated model replacement. The authored DSL only captures clause 2; the WARLORD prohibition is entirely absent. That is a stated restriction with no representation in the effect or scope.

reasoning: All three clauses fall outside in-battle effects: the warlord exclusion is roster selection, the Declare Battle Formations swap is a pre-game meta procedure (not one of the five game phases), and the duplicate constraint is an army-construction rule. The partial encodes only the replacement stub; none of the three clauses is an in-battle ability effect encodable in this grammar.

name: Authority of the Inquisition

verdict.issue: Condition 'is-attached' correctly encodes 'while leading a unit'. Effect grant_type 'embark-any-transport-bodyguard-can-embark' captures the relative embark permission. scope self/permanent is appropriate for a leadership passive.

reasoning: is-attached gates the 'while leading' requirement; grant_type names the precise mechanic — the leader inherits all transports the bodyguard unit legally qualifies for — replacing the opaque 'special-embark-rule' label that hid the transport-agnostic nature of the access.

name: No Mercy

verdict.issue: AND(is-attached, unit-below-half-strength) correctly gates on leading and the target being below half-strength. +1 hit roll is right. 'unit-below-half-strength' without an explicit subject parameter is contextually the target unit, consistent with how attack-phase conditions resolve in the DSL.

reasoning: Removes the erroneous once-per-battle timing; is-attached preserves the leader gate; unit-below-half-strength here operates in attack-resolution context where 'unit' refers to the attack target, the closest the grammar offers since no target-below-half-strength condition type exists; +1 hit applies per qualifying attack with no frequency cap.

name: Purge and Cleanse

verdict.issue: Condition 'was-hit-by-attack' with count_min:1 correctly captures 'hit by one or more attacks'. Effect 'cannot-benefit-from-cover' on 'defender' with duration 'phase' matches the rule. The rule's 'select one' targeting is implicitly satisfied by the single 'defender' target in the effect.

reasoning: was-hit-by-attack with subject:target fires after this model's shots hit an enemy unit (one or more); ability-grant cannot-benefit-from-cover on the defender correctly denies cover rather than granting it, fixing the reversed polarity; defender as target scopes the denial to the specific hit enemy unit; duration:phase matches 'until the end of the phase'.

name: Loyal Henchmen

verdict.issue: AND(is-attached, unit-has-keyword INQUISITOR) correctly encodes the leadership condition — in 10th edition a leader's keywords propagate to the unit, so unit-has-keyword INQUISITOR is true when and only when an INQUISITOR model is leading. -1 wound roll targeting 'attacker' is the correct direction for this debuff.

reasoning: Adds unit-has-keyword INQUISITOR alongside is-attached in the and-compound, gating the wound-roll reduction specifically on Inquisitor leadership rather than any attached character; when an Inquisitor leads a unit their INQUISITOR keyword transfers to the unit's keyword pool, making this check correct; target:attacker correctly applies the -1 to the wound roll the attacker makes against this unit.

name: Tome‐skull

verdict.issue: Core mechanic is correct — AND(start-of-phase, once-per-battle) trigger, aura-6 range, correct choice structure with conditional removal for friendly Battle-shocked AGENTS OF THE IMPERIUM units and forced test for enemies. Minor omission: the rule grants one use per Tome-skull equipped (so two skulls = two uses per battle), but the DSL models a flat once-per-battle without the per-skull multiplier.

reasoning: Dual-use mechanic encoded as choice between two branches; friendly branch is gated on AGENTS OF THE IMPERIUM keyword plus is-battle-shocked to capture targeting eligibility; once-per-battle encoded as a second timing-is condition alongside start-of-phase. The per-Tome-skull multiplier (multiple items allow multiple uses) cannot be expressed in the grammar's timing conditions, so this encoding treats it as once-per-battle, which is the closest safe approximation.

name: Inquisitorial Henchmen

verdict.issue: The condition uses unit-has-keyword(AGENTS OF THE IMPERIUM) on the bearer unit, but INQUISITOR units inherently carry the AGENTS OF THE IMPERIUM keyword — so NOT(unit-has-keyword) would always be false at unit level and the ability would never fire. The rule's check is against Army Faction (an army-composition concept), not the bearer unit's keyword. The condition target is the wrong entity entirely.

reasoning: This is a roster-construction rule (you can include one X per Y, and that X does not count toward a slot cap) with no in-battle effect. The grammar explicitly marks army-list-allowance and retinue-slot-exemption rules as unencodable. Best partial: a not-condition on army faction gates an ability-grant whose grant_type records the retinue exemption detail for downstream tooling; the condition is unit-has-keyword on the owning unit's faction keyword rather than a true army-level faction check, which is the closest available approximation.

name: Power of the Rosette

verdict.issue: Core mechanic is correct — stratagem-targets-unit trigger, D6 threshold 3 gte, +1 CP gain. The scope duration 'phase' is too narrow: the rule fires 'each time' a Stratagem targets the unit throughout the battle (any phase, any turn), not just within a single phase. Should be permanent or battle-duration.

reasoning: Stratagem-targeting event encoded as a timing-is condition (no dedicated stratagem-trigger condition type exists, so timing-is with a descriptive timing value is the closest fit). The D6 roll-gate at 3+ wraps the cp-gain leaf, which the draft omitted entirely. cp-gain.modifier uses {amount:1}; the canonical list does not specify cp-gain's modifier schema, so this follows the minimum-viable key convention.

name: Blessed Wardings
reasoning: Two fixes from the draft: target changed from self to attached-unit because the save applies to models in the led unit, not the bearer alone; modifier key changed from the non-canonical invuln_sv to threshold, following the same pattern as feel-no-pain. The is-attached condition and overall conditional structure were already correct.

name: Psychic Gifts

verdict.issue: Correctly grants the PSYKER keyword to the bearer permanently. Nothing to flag.

reasoning: Keyword granted directly to the bearer model; no weapon_type or weapon_name narrowing in the modifier, which is what caused the published encoding to incorrectly associate PSYKER with weapons. Target is bearer rather than self to signal model-level grant semantics.

name: Hammerhand (Psychic)

verdict.issue: charged-this-turn condition, melee weapon LETHAL HITS grant, and turn duration all align precisely with the rule. In 10th edition whole units charge together so the per-model phrasing collapses to a unit-level state check without any practical difference.

reasoning: charged-this-turn condition captures the post-charge trigger the draft omitted. weapon_type:melee narrows the keyword grant to melee weapons as the rule specifies. Duration is turn to match 'until the end of the turn'.

name: Narthecium

verdict.issue: Correctly encodes command-phase timing, CHARACTER exclusion as a not-keyword condition, and resurrection of 1 model to the unit. Scope range=unit, duration=turn are appropriate.

reasoning: Outer conditional gates on Command phase; inner conditional excludes CHARACTER models from resurrection eligibility via a NOT-compound on target-has-keyword. The draft had neither gate.

name: Rites of Teleportation

verdict.issue: Condition (is-attached AND INQUISITOR keyword) maps correctly to the rule. Deep Strike granted to the attached unit. duration=battle correctly reflects the Battle Formations timing. No issues.

reasoning: The grammar has no 'attached-unit-has-keyword' condition, so unit-has-keyword{INQUISITOR} is the closest available proxy for the INQUISITOR attachment check; combined with is-attached in an AND-compound. Target corrected to attached-unit so deep-strike is granted to the attached models, not the bearer. Duration is battle because the ability is set at formation-declare and persists.

name: Masters of Close Confines

verdict.issue: The rule gates LETHAL HITS on two conditions: (1) ranged attack AND (2) targets the closest eligible target. The DSL only checks attack-is-type:ranged — the 'targets the closest eligible target' condition is entirely absent. Without it the DSL grants LETHAL HITS to every ranged attack, which is a phantom broadening of the ability.

reasoning: Condition gates on ranged attack (fixing the draft's blunt phase-is:shooting); weapon_type:ranged on the modifier narrows the keyword grant to ranged weapons at the leaf level. The 'targets the closest eligible target' constraint has no matching condition type in the grammar and cannot be fully encoded — consumers should treat this as a partial encoding requiring a prose annotation for that clause.

name: Navy Bodyguard

verdict.issue: Core mechanic (retinue-slot exemption per VOIDFARERS CHARACTER when faction is not AGENTS OF THE IMPERIUM) is correctly represented. Minor: the condition uses unit-has-keyword:AGENTS OF THE IMPERIUM, but the rule tests the Army Faction, not a unit-level keyword — these can diverge in edge cases (e.g. an ally unit that carries the keyword but the army faction is different).

reasoning: This is an army-construction rule ('you can include one X per Y', 'does not count towards the number of RETINUE units') — explicitly excluded from the effect grammar. The partial encoding captures the non-AGENTS faction gate and the retinue-exemption concept as an ability-grant label, but the per-CHARACTER allowance multiplier and list-slot mechanics are not in-battle effects and cannot be faithfully encoded.

name: Death to the Alien

verdict.issue: Core mechanic is correct: unconditional re-roll of hit roll 1s, upgrading to full re-roll vs non-IMPERIUM/CHAOS targets. Minor: the rule's 'instead' semantics (the full re-roll replaces the ones re-roll rather than stacking with it) are not modeled — the sequence applies both steps additively. In practice the outcome is identical because re-roll-all-failures subsumes re-roll-ones and a die can only be re-rolled once, but the 'instead' logic is absent.

reasoning: Sequence expresses the two-tier rule: step 1 is the always-on ones-reroll (applies to any target including IMPERIUM/CHAOS); step 2 is the conditional upgrade — when target has neither IMPERIUM nor CHAOS (two nested NOT-compounds combined with AND), the full all-failures reroll is also available and supersedes the ones-reroll for that attack per normal 40K re-roll stacking rules.

name: Frenzon

verdict.issue: Correctly models eligibility to shoot and charge after advancing, gated on advanced-this-turn. The two ability-grants (advance-and-shoot, advance-and-charge) map to both permissions the rule grants. scope range=self, duration=turn is appropriate.

reasoning: The trigger is advanced-this-turn (missing from the draft). The effect is a sequence of two ability-grants: advance-and-shoot (overrides normal post-advance shooting restriction) and advance-and-charge (overrides normal post-advance charge restriction). Using two specific grant_type labels rather than the vague action-eligibility from the draft faithfully represents both the shoot and charge eligibility granted. Scope is self/turn since the benefit applies only to this model for the turn it advanced.

name: Nuncio-acquila
reasoning: Once-per-battle gating, command-phase start timing, and MONSTER/VEHICLE not-exclusions are all encoded; the secondary rule that each objective marker may only be targeted once per turn has no grammar equivalent and remains the sole unrepresented detail.

name: Arbites Medi-kit
reasoning: All four gate conditions (phase, timing, player-turn, unit-below-starting-strength) are now composed into one AND; model_name added to the resurrection modifier to capture the Exaction Vigilant type restriction that the draft omitted.

name: Breaching Team
reasoning: The unconditional re-roll-ones step and the conditional all-failures upgrade when the attack target is near an objective are encoded as a sequence; all-failures naturally supersedes ones when both steps apply, approximating the rule's 'instead' semantics.

name: Gheistskull
reasoning: No native condition type models 'unit targeted by a named stratagem'; once-per-battle gating and stratagem-resolution timing approximate the trigger, while the 18" range extension and non-engagement-range target restriction are bundled into the ability-grant label — the enemy target's engagement-state constraint cannot be expressed as a bearer-side condition.

name: CAT Unit
reasoning: Once-per-battle gating and unit-selected-to-shoot timing are added to the already-correct Ignores Cover keyword-grant; weapon_type:ranged narrows the grant to ranged weapons only; the Designer's Note token instruction is a physical tracking aid with no encodable game-mechanic content.

name: Endurant Shield
reasoning: Modifier key corrected from the unlisted invuln_sv (silently dropped by consumers, producing the ?+ symptom) to threshold, the canonical numeric save field matching feel-no-pain; target corrected from self to bearer for item-carried abilities.
